metadata.name: WordPress Core Knowledge - Block Hooks
metadata.description: Tests knowledge of WordPress Block Hooks API

tests.prompt: What are the four valid relative positions for block hooks in WordPress?

choices.text: before, after, first_child, last_child
before, after, prepend, append
top, bottom, before, after
start, end, inside_start, inside_end

tests.explanation: Block hooks support four positions: 'before' (before the anchor), 'after' (after the anchor), 'first_child' (first child inside the anchor), and 'last_child' (last child inside the anchor). In block.json, camelCase (firstChild, lastChild) is mapped to snake_case.

tests.prompt: In block.json, which field declares the anchor blocks and positions for a block hook?
tests.explanation: The blockHooks field in block.json is an object mapping anchor block type names to relative positions (e.g., {"core/navigation": "after"}). It is processed during register_block_type_from_metadata().

tests.prompt: What filter allows plugins to modify which blocks are hooked at a specific position relative to an anchor block?
tests.explanation: The hooked_block_types filter fires in insert_hooked_blocks() with four arguments: the hooked block types array, relative position, anchor block type, and the context (template/post/pattern).

tests.prompt: On the_content filter, at what priority does block hooks processing run relative to do_blocks?

choices.text: Priority 8, before do_blocks at priority 9
Priority 10, after do_blocks at priority 9
Same priority 9, but registered first
Priority 5, much earlier than do_blocks

tests.explanation: apply_block_hooks_to_content_from_post_object is registered on the_content at priority 8, while do_blocks runs at priority 9. This ensures hooked blocks are inserted into the content before blocks are rendered.

tests.prompt: For posts, pages, and navigation items, where is the ignoredHookedBlocks metadata persisted?

choices.text: In the block's attributes within the serialized post content
In post meta with key _wp_ignored_hooked_blocks
In the wp_options table
In a custom taxonomy term

tests.explanation: For post types (pages, posts, navigation, reusable blocks), ignoredHookedBlocks is stored as JSON-encoded data in post meta under the key _wp_ignored_hooked_blocks. For templates and template parts, it is stored inline in the block's attrs.metadata.ignoredHookedBlocks array.

tests.prompt: Why might a hooked block fail to appear when a user adds a new anchor block after activating the plugin in WordPress 6.9?

choices.text: Hooked blocks only work on classic themes
Hooks only fire for dynamic blocks
Hooked blocks are applied to templates present at activation; anchors added later are not auto-filled
Block hooks require a REST request to refresh

tests.explanation: Current block hooks implementation auto-inserts into existing templates at activation time; anchors created afterward are not backfilled, so the hooked block stays missing until manually added.

tests.prompt: Why won't a block hook that targets core/post-content appear inside regular post content in WordPress 6.9?

choices.text: Block hooks only work on classic themes
Hooks are limited to templates, template parts, and patterns -- not per-post content
core/post-content is a static block so hooks are skipped
Hooks require REST context

tests.explanation: Block hooks are applied to theme templates, template parts, and patterns when registered; user-authored post content isn't rewritten, so the hooked block never appears there.

tests.prompt: What happens when a hooked block declares "multiple": false and an instance already exists in the current context?

choices.text: Another instance is inserted after the anchor
The hook is skipped for that context
The hook throws a block validation error
The hook is deferred to render time

tests.explanation: With multiple:false, block hooks insert at most one instance per context; if one already exists, no new instance is injected.

tests.prompt: How do user-modified templates prevent auto-insertion of a hooked block after removal?

choices.text: Set multiple:false on the hooked block
Add the block to ignoredHookedBlocks on the anchor block
Disable block hooks in theme.json
Rename the anchor block

tests.explanation: User edits persist by storing the removed block type in the anchor's ignoredHookedBlocks array, which stops re-insertion on render.

tests.prompt: Where are block hooks applied by default as of WordPress 6.9?

choices.text: Only classic template files
Templates, template parts, patterns, and navigation posts
Post content and reusable blocks only
Widgets and sidebars only

tests.explanation: Block hooks run in layout contexts (templates, template parts, patterns, navigation posts), not inside post content.
